The '''''Steel Fist''''' was a heavily-modified [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] owned and operated by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Kolig]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The [[Light freighter/Legends|craft]] also served as the main [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] and [[Command ship/Legends|command base]] of [[Qulok's Fist]], a small group of criminals who engaged in [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work. The ship was used by the gang throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], and the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. Well-armed, it was a considerable threat to smaller ships and [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] vessels.

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Qulok's Fist used the [[Freighter/Legends|vessel]] to tail the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' on the orders of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who had been [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]]. Shortly after being contracted for that work, the ''Steel Fist'' [[Skirmish at Shintel|briefly engaged]] the ''FarStar'' in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]] as part of a trap for the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] set by Sarne. Following the terms of the agreement, the ''Steel Fist'' only harassed the ''FarStar'', causing limited damage before retreating.

==Description==
The ''Steel Fist'' was a heavily-modified [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]]. The main [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] was located over the port forward mandible, and the [[Rectenna/Legends|sensor dish]] was on the starboard side.<ref name="tdc" /> Although standard [[YT-series/Legends|YT]]-1300 models were 34.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long,<ref name="blueprints">''[[Star Wars Blueprints: The Ultimate Collection]]''</ref> the ''Steel Fist'' measured only 26.7 meters. In addition, the semi-circular rear [[Engine/Legends|engine]] housing had been removed and replaced by two large engines that were mounted to the stern of the [[Light freighter/Legends|vessel]]. These modifications gave the ''Steel Fist'' a distinctive profile.<ref name="tdc" />

The [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] owner, [[Kolig]], had considerably adapted the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] with reinforced [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating, [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]], and increased maneuverability. Armed with two [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]] and a [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|proton torpedo tube]] with a magazine of six [[Proton torpedo/Legends|warheads]], the vessel was more than capable of engaging in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] and intimidating [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] craft. While the proton torpedoes were designed to be launched from a separate [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] position, the weapon could also be fired from the cockpit, although this greatly reduced the effectiveness of the [[Computer/Legends|computer's]] internal fire-control programs.<ref name="tdc" />

A Class One [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] allowed the freighter to quickly traverse [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with a Class Ten backup for emergencies, and the ship had a top speed of 800 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]]/[[Standard hour/Legends|h]] in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The ship was capable of carrying seventy-five [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo and could store consumables to sustain the crew for two [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. The ship was designed to be crewed by a total of four beings: one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and three gunners. It could also support four passengers.<ref name="tdc" />

==History==
The ''Steel Fist'' was the personal vessel of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] Kolig and the primary spaceship of [[Qulok's Fist]], a group of [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] who also performed [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work and other illegal endeavors. The group's membership included the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Death/Legends|murderer]] [[Sufar]]; the armored warrior [[Padak]]; and [[Thanis Gul-Rah]], a former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]]. The vessel had been extensively modified by Kolig over a number of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] and served Qulok's Fist as its [[Command ship/Legends|base of operations]] and [[Transport/Legends|transport]] for missions throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], and the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]].<ref name="tdc" />

[[File:Steel_Fist_and_FarStar.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The ''Steel Fist'' and the FarStar'' engage in combat in the Shintel system.]]
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="tdc" /> following the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|fall]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Qulok's Fist was contracted by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]—the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]] who had broken from the Empire following the death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]—to trail the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]''.<ref name="tdc" /> The ''FarStar'' was on a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to locate Sarne and his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] following the Moff's flight from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of Kal'Shebbol.<ref name="tsb" /> The ''Steel Fist'' was employed to track the New Republic [[Corvette/Legends|vessel]]<ref name="tdc" /> and was later used by Qulok's Fist to [[Skirmish at Shintel|attack]] the ''FarStar'' in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]].<ref name="sd" />

Sarne had arranged for the ''FarStar'' to divert to [[Shintel]] by using an undercover agent on the planet of [[Gandle Ott]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Olaver Lansel]], to pass on falsified information to the ''FarStar''{{'s}} command crew. The information implied that Sarne was heading for Shintel after departing Gandle Ott, and the ''FarStar'' crew duly followed the lead.<ref name="dirp">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> At Shintel, the vessel encountered an [[Imperial Depot 4290-1|Imperial base]] that had been abandoned by Sarne, who left most of the base personnel stranded as well, because he felt that they were more loyal to the Empire proper than to him personally. Another agent of Sarne's, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Palme]], managed to upload a computer virus into the ''FarStar''{{'s}} systems via a navigational [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] that he claimed was a copy of Sarne's [[Map/Legends|maps]] of the Kathol sector. While the information on the card appeared genuine, it contained triggers that activated [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] trapdoors aboard the ''FarStar'' that caused the corvette's hyperdrive to blow out when the crew attempted to engage it to leave the [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="sd" />

While the ''FarStar'' was stranded, Qulok's Fist used the ''Steel Fist'' to conduct several fast attack runs on the ship before withdrawing once the corvette appeared to be recovering from the initial surprise.<ref name="sd" /> This was on Sarne's orders, as the Moff's ultimate plan for the ''FarStar'' relied on the ship following him, then calling in a larger New Republic force to engage Sarne and his forces. The warlord then intended to destroy these forces in one battle, allowing him to take back control of the Kathol sector unopposed.<ref name="ko">''[[The Kathol Outback]]''</ref>

==Owners and operators==
[[File:QuloksFistMembers-TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|270px|The members of Qulok's Fist, from left to right: Thanis Gul-Rah, Kolig, Padak, and Sufar]]
The ''Steel Fist'' was owned and flown by Kolig, a well-presented bounty hunter. He was the unofficial leader of the criminal group known as Qulok's Fist, which used the vessel as a command base and transport. Other members of the group included Padak, Sufar, and Thanis Gul-Rah, who assisted in crewing the ship. By 8 ABY, Gul-Rah had left the group, leaving only Kolig, Sufar, and Padak to man the vessel.<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Steel Fist'' was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]], as the ship and base of operations for the Qulok's Fist bounty hunter gang. It appeared in the "Rogues Gallery" section, written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], and was included so the gamemaster could use it—and Qulok's Fist—in an original adventure. The ''Steel Fist'' would go on to appear in the adventure ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', written by [[Paul Sudlow]].

During the ''Steel Fist''{{'s}} encounter with the ''FarStar'' at Shintel, the battle can be played out in several ways. The player characters may use an [[Mark III Merr-Sonn Defender Ion Mine|ion minefield]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] Shintel to their advantage by leading their opponents into it and successfully piloting close to the [[Mine/Legends|mines]] in an attempt to disable their attackers. If, however, the players are struggling to recover from the initial attack, the gamemaster can have Colonel [[Heget]], the Imperial commander of the supply depot on the planet, order his [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] into battle, or have them rescued by the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] adventurer [[Kyli Ned'Ix]]. This article makes no assumptions as to which course of action is chosen by the players.

Players may also avoid the encounter with the ''Steel Fist'' by simply not uploading Lieutenant Palme's datacard. However, the adventure continues on the assumption that this has happened, as it leads into the events of the second part of the adventure. This article assumes the encounter happened as scripted in the adventure.
